name: cl-moe-continual
description: Prevent catastrophic forgetting in continual learning via task-specific expert modules. Each task gets dedicated MoE expert + LoRA adapter, preventing interference. Routers learn to select appropriate experts per task.
paperTitle: CL-MoE: Enhancing Continual Learning with Mixture of Experts and Task-Specific Adapters
keywords: [continual learning, mixture of experts, catastrophic forgetting, task adapters]
content:
## CL-MoE: Experts for Continual Learning

Continual learning suffers catastrophic forgetting when learning new tasks. MoE with task-specific experts prevents interference.

## Architecture

```
New Task → Router → Select Expert E_t → Task-Specific Adapter → Output
                    (learned per task)

Old Tasks still use E_1, E_2, ... (no interference)
```

## Implementation

```python
class CLMoE(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_tasks=10, expert_dim=256):
        super().__init__()
        self.experts = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(256, expert_dim), nn.ReLU())
            for _ in range(num_tasks)
        ])
        self.routers = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.Linear(256, num_tasks) for _ in range(num_tasks)
        ])

    def forward(self, x, task_id):
        # Route to expert for this task
        expert = self.experts[task_id]
        return expert(x)

def train_continual(model, new_task_data, old_task_data):
    # Train new expert
    new_expert = model.experts[current_task]
    loss_new = mse(new_expert(new_task_data), targets)

    # Regularize to prevent forgetting old tasks
    loss_old = sum(
        mse(model.experts[t](old_task_data[t]), old_targets[t])
        for t in range(current_task)
    )

    total_loss = loss_new + 0.1 * loss_old
    total_loss.backward()
```
